caption: Axial DWI image shows no restricted diffusion . Although CCs usually have viscid gelatinous material, they do not show restricted diffusion. ADC values are often higher than that of normal cerebral parenchyma and lower than CSF.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

cleanCaption: Axial DWI image shows no restricted diffusion . Although CCs usually have viscid gelatinous material, they do not show restricted diffusion. ADC values are often higher than that of normal cerebral parenchyma and lower than CSF.

caption: Coronal T2 MR in the same patient demonstrates a T2-hyperintense mass with an eccentric T2-hypointense intracystic nodule . Intracystic nodule dot sign has been described in CC similar to a Rathke cleft cyst. T2 hypointensity is likely due to free radical-containing compounds. CCs and Rathke cleft cysts are endodermal in origin.
